MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Maria Bourn (maria@attorneytanya.com) for Plaintiff.
Amber Eklof (aeklof@grsm.com) for specially appearing individual Defendant, Ick Soo Pang.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ICK PANG's MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS:

Specially appearing defendant Pang's motion to quash for lack of personal jurisdiction is granted. "Although defendant is the moving party, the burden of proof is on the plaintiff: "[W]hen jurisdiction is challenged by a nonresident defendant, the burden of proof is upon the plaintiff to demonstrate that 'minimum contacts' exist between defendant and the forum state to justify imposition of personal jurisdiction." (Mihlon v. Superior Court (1985) 169 Cal.App.3d 703, 710.) Plaintiff does not show that Mr. Pang is subject to personal jurisdiction in California, as Plaintiff does not show that Mr. Pang is subject to general jurisdiction or that Mr. Pang had individual contacts with California that would subject him to jurisdiction.

An individual's "contacts with California are not to be judged according to [her] employer's activities there . . . Each defendant's contacts with the forum State must be assessed individually." (Calder v. Jones (1984) 465 U.S. 783, 790.) Plaintiff does not show that Mr. Pang is subject to general jurisdiction. Mr. Pang is domiciled in Colorado. (Pang Decl. 2-3.)

Plaintiff also does not show that Mr. Pang is subject to specific jurisdiction. "[I]t is well established by California case law that for jurisdictional purposes the acts of corporate officers and directors, in their official capacities, are acts exclusively of . . . the corporation, and are thus not material for purposes of establishing minimum contacts as to individuals . . . acts performed by individuals in such official capacities may not reasonably be attributed to them as individual acts creating personal jurisdiction." (Mihlon, 169 Cal.App.3d at 713.)

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
